title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani)
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Indian Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) with our easy, authentic recipe. Creamy, rich, and flavorful – perfect for a homemade feast!
longTailKeywords: butter chicken recipe step by step
lowCompetitionKeywords: restaurant style butter chicken at home
h2Headings: Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic butter chicken (murgh makhani) served with rice and naan bread, garnished with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our authentic Classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) recipe.

description: Experience the rich, creamy decadence of Classic Butter Chicken, a beloved Indian dish that tantalizes the taste buds with its tender chicken simmered in a luscious tomato-based sauce. This Murgh Makhani recipe promises an authentic culinary journey, balancing sweet, tangy, and subtly spicy flavors for a truly unforgettable meal.

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste, lemon juice, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, 1/2 tsp garam masala, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or up to 4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter and 1 tbsp oil in a large pan or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 3-4 minutes per side until lightly browned and slightly charred. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt 1 tbsp butter. Add the chopped onion and cook until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1/2 tbsp ginger-garlic paste and cook for another minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed tomatoes, the remaining 1/2 tsp garam masala, and sugar. Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken slightly. Stir occasionally.

instructions.title: Blend the Sauce
instructions.text: Drain the soaked cashews. Carefully transfer the tomato mixture to a blender along with the cashews. Blend until completely smooth and creamy. For an extra silky sauce, you can strain it through a fine-mesh sieve, pressing down on the solids.

instructions.title: Combine and Finish
instructions.text: Return the smooth sauce to the pan. Add the cooked chicken pieces. Stir in the remaining 2 tbsp butter, heavy cream, and crushed fenugreek leaves (Kasuri Methi). Simmer on low heat for another 5-7 minutes, allowing the chicken to warm through and absorb the sauce flavors. Do not boil after adding cream.

instructions.title: Serve Hot
instructions.text: Taste and adjust seasoning if needed. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and serve hot with naan bread, basmati rice, or roti. Enjoy your homemade classic Butter Chicken!

tips: For an authentic smoky flavor, char the chicken slightly under a broiler or on a grill before adding to the sauce.
If you dont have Kashmiri red chili powder, use regular chili powder but reduce the amount as it is spicier. You can also add a pinch of red food coloring for the classic vibrant red hue.
Always add cream and butter at the very end and avoid vigorous boiling afterward to prevent the sauce from splitting and maintain its velvety texture.
article:
Butter Chicken, known in India as Murgh Makhani, is more than just a dish; it's a global culinary phenomenon. This iconic Indian chicken recipe has captivated palates worldwide with its rich, creamy tomato-based sauce and tender, succulent chicken pieces. Originating from Delhi in the 1950s, Butter Chicken quickly rose to fame and remains a staple in Indian restaurants and homes alike. Its mild, slightly sweet, and tangy profile makes it incredibly approachable, even for those new to Indian cuisine, while its complexity of flavors keeps aficionados coming back for more.

If you've ever dreamed of recreating that restaurant-quality Butter Chicken experience in your own kitchen, you're in the right place. This comprehensive guide will walk you through everything you need to know, from selecting the right ingredients to mastering the techniques that yield the most authentic and delicious Murgh Makhani. Prepare to impress your family and friends with this timeless classic.

## What is Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani)?

Butter Chicken, or Murgh Makhani, literally translates to "chicken with butter." It's a delectable Indian curry characterized by its velvety smooth texture, vibrant orange-red hue, and a harmonious balance of sweet, tangy, and mildly spicy flavors. The dish typically involves marinated chicken, often grilled or pan-fried, submerged in a rich gravy made from tomatoes, cream, butter, and a blend of aromatic spices. The secret to its distinctive taste lies in the careful layering of flavors – from the initial tempering of spices to the slow simmering of the tomato base and the final touch of butter and fresh cream. Unlike some spicier Indian curries, Butter Chicken is known for its gentle warmth, making it a universal favorite. Its history traces back to the Moti Mahal restaurant in Delhi, where it was created by Kundan Lal Jaggi and Kundan Lal Gujral, originally as a way to use leftover tandoori chicken.

## Ingredients You Need

Crafting the perfect Butter Chicken requires a selection of fresh, high-quality ingredients for both the chicken marinade and the rich, luxurious sauce.

For the Chicken Marinade:
*   500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts, cut into 1-inch pieces
*   1/2 cup plain yogurt
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 tsp red chili powder (Kashmiri chili powder for color, less heat)
*   1/2 tsp turmeric powder
*   1/2 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp salt
*   1 tbsp lemon juice

For the Makhani Sauce:
*   2 tbsp unsalted butter
*   1 tbsp oil (vegetable or canola)
*   1 large onion, finely chopped
*   1 tbsp ginger-garlic paste
*   1 can (400g) crushed tomatoes or 3-4 ripe medium tomatoes, pureed
*   1/4 cup raw cashews (soaked in hot water for 15 minutes, then drained)
*   1/2 tsp red chili powder
*   1 tsp coriander powder
*   1/2 tsp cumin powder
*   1/4 tsp turmeric powder
*   1/2 tsp garam masala
*   1/2 tsp sugar (or to taste, balances acidity)
*   1/2 tsp salt (or to taste)
*   1/2 cup water or chicken broth
*   1/4 cup heavy cream (plus more for garnish)
*   1 tsp kasuri methi (dried fenugreek leaves), crushed
*   Fresh cilantro, chopped (for garnish)

## Step-by-Step Instructions

Follow these steps to create an authentic and mouthwatering Butter Chicken:

1.  **Marinate the Chicken**: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients for the chicken marinade. Add the chicken pieces, toss to coat thoroughly, cover, and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or ideally 2-4 hours for maximum flavor.
2.  **Cook the Chicken**: Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large skillet or non-stick pan over medium-high heat. Remove chicken from marinade, shaking off excess. Cook chicken in batches until browned and cooked through, about 5-7 minutes per side. Set aside. You can also grill or bake the chicken for a more authentic tandoor-like flavor.
3.  **Prepare the Sauce Base**: In the same pan (or a separate pot), melt 2 tbsp butter with 1 tbsp oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes.
4.  **Add Aromatics and Spices**: Stir in the ginger-garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Add red chili powder, coriander powder, cumin powder, and turmeric powder. Sauté for 30 seconds, stirring constantly, to bloom the spices.
5.  **Build the Sauce**: Pour in the crushed tomatoes (or tomato puree) and the soaked cashews. Stir well. Add sugar and salt. Bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavors to meld and the tomatoes to cook down.
6.  **Blend for Smoothness**: Carefully transfer the entire sauce mixture to a blender. Blend until completely smooth. For an extra silky sauce, you can pass it through a fine-mesh sieve back into the pot.
7.  **Combine and Simmer**: Return the smooth sauce to the pot over medium-low heat. Add water or chicken broth to adjust consistency to your preference. Stir in the cooked chicken pieces.
8.  **Finish with Cream and Kasuri Methi**: Simmer for another 5-10 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the sauce's flavors. Stir in the heavy cream and crushed kasuri methi. Cook for just 1-2 minutes more, ensuring the sauce doesn't boil after adding the cream.
9.  **Garnish and Serve**: Taste and adjust seasonings if necessary. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro and a drizzle of extra cream or a small pat of butter.

## Pro Tips for the Best Results

Achieving that perfect, restaurant-quality Butter Chicken is all about a few key techniques and ingredient choices:

*   **Marination Time is Key**: Don't skimp on marinating the chicken. The longer it marinates (up to 24 hours), the more tender and flavorful your chicken will be, absorbing all those delicious spices.
*   **Quality Tomatoes**: Use good quality canned crushed tomatoes or fresh, ripe, juicy tomatoes. The tomatoes form the backbone of the sauce, so their flavor is paramount.
*   **Soak Cashews for Creaminess**: Soaking cashews makes them easier to blend into a super smooth paste, contributing significantly to the sauce's luxurious texture without adding too much fat. You can also use blanched almonds for a similar effect.
*   **Blend Thoroughly**: A truly smooth sauce is crucial for authentic Murgh Makhani. Blend until there are no discernible pieces of onion or cashew. A high-speed blender is your friend here. For the silkiest texture, pass it through a sieve.
*   **Kashmiri Chili Powder**: This specific chili powder provides a beautiful red color without adding excessive heat, which is characteristic of traditional Butter Chicken. If you can't find it, use regular red chili powder sparingly and adjust to taste.
*   **Kasuri Methi (Dried Fenugreek Leaves)**: This ingredient is non-negotiable for authentic flavor. Crush it between your palms before adding to release its aroma. It adds a unique, slightly bitter, and earthy depth.
*   **Balance Flavors**: Taste your sauce at the end. The sugar balances the acidity of the tomatoes, while salt and garam masala bring everything together. Adjust as needed.

## Serving Suggestions

Butter Chicken is a hearty and flavorful dish that pairs wonderfully with a variety of Indian breads and rice.

*   **Naan Bread**: The classic accompaniment. Warm, fluffy naan is perfect for soaking up every last bit of the rich, creamy sauce. Garlic naan or butter naan elevate the experience.
*   **Basmati Rice**: Steamed basmati rice or jeera rice (cumin rice) provides a neutral base that beautifully complements the complex flavors of the Butter Chicken.
*   **Roti or Chapati**: For a lighter option, whole wheat roti or chapati are excellent choices.
*   **Side Salad**: A simple cucumber and onion salad or a raita (yogurt dip) can offer a refreshing contrast to the richness of the curry.
*   **Garnish**: Always finish your homemade Butter Chicken with a sprinkle of fresh chopped cilantro and a swirl of heavy cream for visual appeal and an extra touch of decadence.

## Variations and Substitutions

While the classic Butter Chicken recipe is perfect as is, there are several ways to adapt it to dietary needs or personal preferences:

*   **Dairy-Free**: Replace yogurt with a plant-based alternative (like coconut yogurt or almond yogurt) for the marinade. Substitute heavy cream with full-fat coconut milk or cashew cream, and use a dairy-free butter alternative.
*   **Vegetarian Murgh Makhani**: For a delicious vegetarian version, substitute chicken with paneer (Indian cheese), tofu, or even chickpeas. Marinate paneer or tofu similarly and pan-fry until golden before adding to the sauce.
*   **Adjusting Spice Level**: If you prefer more heat, increase the amount of red chili powder or add a finely chopped green chili to the sauce base. For less heat, reduce the chili powder or omit it entirely and rely on the garam masala for warmth.
*   **Smoky Flavor**: If you want a smoky flavor reminiscent of tandoor-cooked chicken but don't have a tandoor, you can use a small piece of charcoal. Heat it until red hot, place it in a small bowl in the pot with the chicken and sauce, drizzle a little ghee or oil over it, and quickly cover the pot for 5-10 minutes.
*   **Healthier Options**: Use leaner chicken breast, reduce the amount of butter and cream, and increase the vegetable content by adding pureed carrots or bell peppers to the sauce for extra nutrients and color.

Embark on this culinary journey and create your own unforgettable Butter Chicken. With these detailed instructions and tips, you're well on your way to mastering one of India's most beloved dishes right in your own kitchen!
